title: The Benefits of Being Bilingual
content_html:
The Benefits of Being Bilingual

A. According to the latest figures, the majority of the world’s population is now bilingual or multilingual, having grown up speaking two or more languages. In the past, such children were considered to be at a disadvantage compared with their monolingual peers. Over the past few decades, however, technological advances have allowed researchers to look more deeply at how bilingualism interacts with and changes the cognitive and neurological systems, thereby identifying several clear benefits of being bilingual.

B. Research shows that when a bilingual person uses one language, the other is active at the same time. When we hear a word, we don’t hear the entire word all at once: the sounds arrive in sequential order. Long before the word is finished, the brain’s language system begins to guess what that word might be. If you hear ‘can’, you will likely activate words like ‘candy’ and ‘candle’ as well, at least during the earlier stages of word recognition. For bilingual people, this activation is not limited to a single language; auditory input activates corresponding words regardless of the language to which they belong. Some of the most compelling evidence for this phenomenon, called ‘language co-activation’, comes from studying eye movements. A Russian-English bilingual asked to ‘pick up a marker’ from a set of objects would look more at a stamp than someone who doesn’t know Russian, because the Russian word for ‘stamp’, marka, sounds like the English word he or she heard, ‘marker’. In cases like this, language co-activation occurs because what the listener hears could map onto words in either language.

C. Having to deal with this persistent linguistic competition can result in difficulties, however. For instance, knowing more than one language can cause speakers to name pictures more slowly, and can increase ‘tip-of-the-tongue states’, when you can almost, but not quite, bring a word to mind. As a result, the constant juggling of two languages creates a need to control how much a person accesses a language at any given time. For this reason, bilingual people often perform better on tasks that require conflict management. In the classic Stroop Task, people see a word and are asked to name the colour of the word’s font. When the colour and the word match (i.e., the word ‘red’ printed in red), people correctly name the colour more quickly than when the colour and the word don’t match (i.e., the word ‘red’ printed in blue). This occurs because the word itself (‘red’) and its font colour (blue) conflict. Bilingual people often excel at tasks such as this, which top into the ability to ignore competing perceptual information and focus on the relevant aspects of the input. Bilinguals are also better at switching between two tasks; for example, when bilinguals have to switch from categorizing objects by colour (red or green) to categorizing them by shape (circle or triangle), they do so more quickly than monolingual people, reflecting better cognitive control when having to make rapid changes of strategy.

D. It also seems that the neurological roots of the bilingual advantage extend to brain areas more traditionally associated with sensory processing. When monolingual and bilingual adolescents listen to simple speech sounds without any intervening background noise, they show highly similar brain stem responses. When researchers play the same sound to both groups in the presence of background noise, however, the bilingual listeners’ neural response is considerably larger, reflecting better encoding of the sound’s fundamental frequency, a feature of sound closely related to pitch perception.

E. Such improvements in cognitive and sensory processing may help a bilingual person to process information in the environment, and help explain why bilingual adults acquire a third language better than monolingual adults master a second language. This advantage may be rooted in the skill of focussing on information about the new language while reducing interference from the languages they already know.

F. Research also indicates that bilingual experience may help to keep the cognitive mechanisms sharp by recruiting alternate brain networks to compensate for those that become damaged during aging. Older bilinguals enjoy improved memory relative to monolingual people, which can lead to real-world health benefits. In a study of over 200 patients with Alzheimer’s disease, a degenerative brain disease, bilingual patients reported showing initial symptoms of the disease an average of five years later than monolingual patients. In a follow-up study, researchers compared the brains of bilingual and monolingual patients matched on the severity of Alzheimer’s symptoms. Surprisingly, the bilinguals’ brains had more physical signs of disease than their monolingual counterparts, even though their outward behaviour and abilities were the same. If the brain is an engine, bilingualism may help it to go farther on the same amount of fuel.

G. Furthermore, the benefits associated with bilingual experience seem to start very early. In one study, researchers taught seven-month-old babies growing up in monolingual or bilingual homes that when they heard a tinkling sound, a puppet appeared on one side of a screen. Halfway through the study, the puppet began appearing on the opposite side of the screen. In order to get a reward, the infants had to adjust the rule they’d learned; only the bilingual babies were able to successfully learn the new rule. This suggests that for very young children, as well as for older people, navigating a multilingual environment imparts advantages that transfer far beyond language.

questions.groupHtml:
Questions 27–31

Complete the table below.

Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Observing, Russian-English bilingual, select certain objects, mechanism known as.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn B: "Some of the most compelling evidence for this phenomenon, called 'language co-activation', comes from studying eye movements. A Russian-English bilingual asked to ‘pick up a marker’..."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Thí nghiệm này quan sát sự chuyển động của mắt ("eye movements") của những người song ngữ Nga-Anh khi chọn đồ vật.

Cơ chế đồng thời kích hoạt cả hai ngôn ngữ này được bài đọc gọi tên trực tiếp là "language co-activation".

Đáp án: eye movements

questions.question_text: Observing the 27. ________ of Russian-English bilingual people when asked to select certain objects

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Bilingual people, engage both languages, simultaneously, mechanism known as.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn B: "Some of the most compelling evidence for this phenomenon, called 'language co-activation', comes from studying eye movements."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Engage both languages simultaneously" (sử dụng cả hai ngôn ngữ cùng một lúc) hoàn toàn đồng nghĩa với định nghĩa của hiện tượng được gọi tên trực tiếp trong bài là language co-activation.

Đáp án: language co-activation
questions.question_text: Bilingual people engage both languages simultaneously: a mechanism known as 28. ________

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

A test called, focusing on naming colours.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn C: "In the classic Stroop Task, people see a word and are asked to name the colour of the word’s font."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Focusing on naming colours" (tập trung vào việc gọi tên màu sắc) chính là nội dung mô tả của bài kiểm tra kinh điển có tên riêng được viết hoa trong đoạn văn: Stroop Task.

Đáp án: Stroop Task
questions.question_text: A test called the 29. ________, focusing on naming colours

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Bilingual people, more able to handle tasks, skill called.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn C: "For this reason, bilingual people often perform better on tasks that require conflict management."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"More able to handle tasks" đồng nghĩa với cụm từ perform better on tasks (làm tốt hơn ở các nhiệm vụ). Kỹ năng giúp họ xử lý được các xung đột thông tin này được gọi là conflict management.

Đáp án: conflict management
questions.question_text: Bilingual people are more able to handle tasks involving a skill called 30. ________

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Changing strategies, bilingual people, superior.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn C: "...reflecting better cognitive control when having to make rapid changes of strategy."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Changing strategies" đồng nghĩa với cụm từ make rapid changes of strategy (thay đổi chiến thuật nhanh chóng). Khi thực hiện việc này, người song ngữ có sự thể hiện vượt trội (better ~ superior) về khả năng cognitive control.

Đáp án: cognitive control
questions.question_text: When changing strategies, bilingual people have superior 31. ________

questions.groupHtml:
Questions 32–36

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in the Reading Passage?

In boxes 32–36 on your answer sheet, write
YES  if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO  if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN  if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Attitudes towards bilingualism, changed, recent years.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn A: "In the past, such children were considered to be at a disadvantage... Over the past few decades, however, [researchers] identifying several clear benefits of being bilingual."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Trong quá khứ (In the past), việc biết hai thứ tiếng bị coi là bất lợi. Tuy nhiên, trong vài thập kỷ gần đây (past few decades ~ recent years), các nghiên cứu lại tìm ra nhiều lợi ích rõ ràng. Điều này chứng minh quan điểm và thái độ xã hội đã thay đổi, khớp hoàn toàn với nhận định của câu hỏi.

Đáp án: YES

_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.question_text: Attitudes towards bilingualism have changed in recent years.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Bilingual people, better than monolingual, guessing correctly, words, before finished.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn B: "Long before the word is finished, the brain’s language system begins to guess what that word might be... For bilingual people, this activation is not limited to a single language..."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Bài đọc có nhắc đến việc bộ não sẽ tự động đưa ra dự đoán về từ ngữ trước khi nghe xong âm thanh (guess... before finished). Tuy nhiên, tác giả chỉ giải thích về cơ chế kích hoạt đồng thời cả hai ngôn ngữ ở người song ngữ, chứ không hề đưa ra so sánh xem họ đoán có "đúng hơn hay giỏi hơn" (better at guessing correctly) người đơn ngữ hay không.

Đáp án: NOT GIVEN
questions.question_text: Bilingual people are better than monolingual people at guessing correctly what words are before they are finished.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Bilingual people, consistently, name images, faster, than monolingual.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn C: "For instance, knowing more than one language can cause speakers to name pictures more slowly..."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Name images" đồng nghĩa với cụm từ name pictures. Bài đọc khẳng định việc biết từ hai ngôn ngữ trở lên sẽ khiến người nói gọi tên các bức tranh chậm hơn (more slowly), điều này trái ngược hoàn toàn với thông tin "nhanh hơn" (faster) mà câu hỏi đưa ra.

Đáp án: NO
questions.question_text: Bilingual people consistently name images faster than monolingual people.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Process single sounds, more efficiently, in all situations.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn D: "When monolingual and bilingual adolescents listen to simple speech sounds without any intervening background noise, they show highly similar brain stem responses."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Câu hỏi khẳng định người song ngữ xử lý âm thanh tốt hơn trong mọi tình huống (in all situations). Tuy nhiên, bài đọc chỉ ra ở tình huống môi trường yên tĩnh không có tạp âm (without background noise), phản ứng não bộ của hai nhóm là giống hệt nhau (highly similar). Họ chỉ xử lý hiệu quả hơn khi có tiếng ồn nền bủa vây. Bản chất thông tin bị mâu thuẫn.

Đáp án: NO
questions.question_text: Bilingual people's brains process single sounds more efficiently than monolingual people in all situations.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Fewer bilingual people, suffer from brain disease, old age.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn F: "In a study of over 200 patients with Alzheimer’s disease... bilingual patients reported showing initial symptoms of the disease an average of five years later than monolingual patients."

Bước 3: Phân tích

Bài đọc đề cập đến việc nghiên cứu trên những người đã bị mắc bệnh Alzheimer và chỉ ra người biết hai thứ tiếng sẽ phát tác các triệu chứng muộn hơn 5 năm. Tác giả không hề cung cấp số liệu so sánh tổng lượng người bị bệnh để kết luận xem liệu nhóm song ngữ có số người mắc bệnh ít hơn (fewer) nhóm đơn ngữ hay không.

Đáp án: NOT GIVEN
questions.question_text: Fewer bilingual people than monolingual people suffer from brain disease in old age.

questions.groupHtml:
Questions 37–40

Reading Passage has sections A–G. Which section contains the following information?

Write the correct letter, A–G, in boxes 37–40 on your answer sheet.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Brains respond differently, certain type, non-verbal auditory input.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn D: "When researchers play the same sound to both groups in the presence of background noise, however, the bilingual listeners’ neural response is considerably larger..."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Auditory input" (tín hiệu âm thanh truyền vào) tương ứng với cụm từ play the same sound. Đoạn văn này làm rõ thí nghiệm đo lường xung thần kinh và chứng minh bộ não của hai nhóm phản ứng khác nhau rõ rệt (neural response is considerably larger) khi nghe âm thanh có tiếng ồn.

Đáp án: D

_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.question_text: an example of how bilingual and monolingual people's brains respond differently to a certain type of non-verbal auditory input

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Bilingual upbringing, benefits, before we learn to speak.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn G: "In one study, researchers taught seven-month-old babies growing up in monolingual or bilingual homes... only the bilingual babies were able to successfully learn the new rule."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Before we learn to speak" (trước khi biết nói) tương ứng chính xác với đối tượng thí nghiệm là các em bé mới chỉ 7 tháng tuổi (seven-month-old babies). Đoạn văn chứng minh việc lớn lên trong môi trường song ngữ đem lại lợi thế về tư duy linh hoạt từ khi còn rất nhỏ.

Đáp án: G
questions.question_text: a demonstration of how a bilingual upbringing has benefits even before we learn to speak

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Process, identify words, they hear.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn B: "When we hear a word, we don’t hear the entire word all at once: the sounds arrive in sequential order. Long before the word is finished, the brain’s language system begins to guess..."

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Identify words" đồng nghĩa với thuật ngữ word recognition (nhận diện từ ngữ). Đoạn văn này giải thích chi tiết cơ chế vận hành của bộ não: âm thanh truyền đến theo trình tự và não bộ sẽ bắt đầu suy đoán, phân tích để xác định từ đó là gì trước khi âm thanh kết thúc.

Đáp án: B
questions.question_text: a description of the process by which people identify words that they hear

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword

Negative consequences, being bilingual.

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn

Đoạn C: "Having to deal with this persistent linguistic competition can result in difficulties, however. For instance, knowing more than one language can cause speakers to name pictures more slowly, and can increase ‘tip-of-the-tongue states’..."  

Bước 3: Phân tích

"Negative consequences" (hệ quả/tác động tiêu cực) tương ứng với cụm từ result in difficulties (dẫn đến những khó khăn). Tác giả đã liệt kê cụ thể các điểm trừ này bao gồm: tốc độ phản xạ gọi tên tranh bị chậm đi và dễ rơi vào trạng thái từ ngữ lẩn khuất ngay đầu lưỡi mà không nhớ ra nổi.

Đáp án: C
questions.question_text: reference to some negative consequences of being bilingual
